---
title: "MC1_proposal"
format: html
author: "Persis Evangeline"
date: "June 2, 2026"
date-modified: "last-modified"
execute: 
  eval: true
  echo: true
  warning: false
  freeze: true
---

# Project title

Investigating the Tenant Thread Embargo Breach: Visual Analytics Approach

# Introduction

TenantThread is a property technology company that provides software solutions for landlords and multifamily operators. 1 of its key products, the Retention Optimiser, analyses tenant behaviours and generates scores that influences operational decisions. 

TenantThread entered into a confidential merger agreement with CivivLoom Realty Partners. This merger announcement was protected by strict embargo until 6:00 PM on 5 June 2046 and monitored by a system known as "The Judge". Despite these safegaurds, the embargoed information appeared publicly on FleX before the official announcement time. 


# Project objectives and aims

The aim of the project is to develop visual analytics system to reconstruct the events leading to the release and determine whether this was caused on purpose, behavioural drift among agents within the system, or a flaw/breakdown within the system. 

The analysis will look into communications, agent deliberations, decision making records and public posts generated during the 2 weeks before the breach. 

Objectives:

1. Reconstruct sequence of events

2. Identify key actors, communication channels, decision points that contribute to the release of information

3. Examine relationships between agents

4. Determine flow of information

5. Make comparisons during normal operations against during crisis period

6. Detect and determine which are the leading indicators that signal the embargo violation

7. Identify if similar patterns have existed before


# Motivation 

The use of AI in organisation is becoming more common and prevalant. Understanding how autonomous systems behave under pressure is important. 

This TenantThread case is a chance to study and analyse how information propagates through a network of agents and small behavioural changes may lead to major organisational failures. Visual Analytics can give us a clearer understanding of the communication flows, dynamics, identify vulnerabilities in the system and develop stronger frameworks for future use cases.


# Challenges

1. Dataset has several layers of communication: public posts, private posts, compliance related interactions. There is a need to be able to differentiate and derive meaningful insights from these. 

2. The chain of events leading to the breach may not be so direct and explicit. There is a need to derive this through further analysis. 

3. Agent behaviour not so straightforward. For instance, was it an accident or done deliberately?

4. Large volume of interactions could lead to unecessary noise which needs to be filtered out.


# Proposed Approach

Start off with data preparation and EDA. This is to understand overall data and get generic trends. Next, create a chronological view of events leading to the breach. This timeline will help identify key moments, series of events and identify developments. Next we would carry out Network analysis and then followed by behavioural analysis. In network analysis, we can view, analyse and deduce the communication pathways and identify the important ones. Behavioural analysis such as communication frequency, interaction diversity and trends can be observed. There is also a need to find anomalies which could help determine cause of breach.  



# Scope of work

| Component | Description | Expected Outcome |
|-----------|-------------|------------------|
| Data Preparation and Exploration | Understand dataset structure, clean and transform communication records, categorise communication channels and interaction types, and identify key entities, events, and timestamps. | A structured dataset ready for subsequent analysis. |
| Network Analysis | Construct communication networks among agents, communication channels, and key entities. Compute network metrics such as degree centrality, betweenness centrality, and community structures to identify influential actors and information bottlenecks. | Identification of key agents, influential decision-makers, communication clusters, and critical pathways through which information propagated. |
| Event Reconstruction | Develop a chronological timeline of significant events, highlight critical decisions and escalation points, and identify events directly related to the merger embargo. | A clear reconstruction of the sequence of events leading to the embargo breach. |
| Agent Relationship Analysis | Analyse communication flows among agents, identify collaboration patterns, and examine how interactions evolved throughout the crisis period. | Better understanding of relationships between agents and how their interactions contributed to the embargo breach. |
| Behavioural Analysis | Establish baseline behaviour for each agent, compare normal and crisis-period activities, and identify changes in communication frequency, content, and interaction patterns. | Identification of behavioural shifts associated with the crisis. |
| Anomaly Detection | Detect unusual behaviours and deviations from expected roles, investigate prior irregularities, and compare historical anomalies with those observed during the breach. | Discovery of abnormal behaviours that may have contributed to the embargo violation. |
| Leading Indicator Analysis | Identify early warning signs preceding the breach, examine recurring risk patterns, and evaluate whether warning signals were visible before the incident occurred. | Insights into indicators that could predict future compliance failures. |
| Visual Analytics Dashboard Development | Integrate timeline, network, behavioural, and anomaly analyses into an interactive system for investigation and exploration. | An interactive visual analytics platform supporting evidence-based decision making. |


# Timeline

<table style="width:100%; border-collapse:collapse;" border="1">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Period</th>
      <th style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Key Activities</th>
      <th style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Deliverables</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">15 – 17 Jun 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Data Preparation and Exploration</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Cleaned dataset, identified key entities, communication channels, and events</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">18 – 20 Jun 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Network Analysis and Event Reconstruction</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Communication network models and preliminary event timeline</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">21 – 24 Jun 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Agent Relationship Analysis and Behavioural Analysis</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Agent interaction findings and behavioural comparison results</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">25 – 27 Jun 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Anomaly Detection and Leading Indicator Analysis</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Identified anomalies, behavioural deviations, and early warning indicators</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">28 – 30 Jun 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Shiny Application Development, Dashboard Integration, and Testing</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Completed visual analytics dashboard and validated application</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">1 Jul 2026</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Poster Preparation, Repository Deployment, Final Review and Submission</td>
      <td style="border:1px solid #4b006e; padding:10px;">Project poster, deployed Shiny application, and final deliverables submitted</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>






# Conclusion

This project aims to develop a visual analytics system to investigate the breach which occurred at TenantThread. This will be done through various types of analysis such as network analysis, behavioural analysis, agent interactions, reconstructing events and more. This will provide insights into how information propagated through the system and whether the breach was an accident or done intentionally. The findings will provide a deeper understanding of AI-agent behaviour and contribute to more effective governance and risk management in complex organisational behaviours.
